profile.markdown:
**Full Name:** Esmeralda

**Alias:** Astrologer

**Born:** Stargate Valley, Agelta Drylands

**Species:** Human

**Gender:** Female

**Occupation:** Astrologer

**Affiliation:** Agelta astrologers

**Weapons:**
- Stardust
- Frostmoon

**Abilities:**
- Shield through the power of the Starmoon Casket
- Astrospace power

Born into a family of Agelta astrologers, she is the greatest astrologers on the Land of Dawn.

Through the treasure star-moon box, she learned the fact that the tyrant Khufra was about to wake up. The re-sealing of Khufra became her new mission as an astrologer.

story.markdown:
"Mysterious astrologer"

The astrologers, who have constantly been observing astrospace, possess the occult abilities to explain and predict everything in the world, including both about their processes and results. To become an astrologer, candidates should pass rigorous assessments, which will test both their willpower and astrological abilities. Going down the generations, the elders finally select Esmeralda, a young girl living in the Western Desert, to be a new successor in the Land of Dawn.

After becoming the successor of the astrologers, Esmeralda guards the Starmoon Casket that has been passed down for generations. With the hope of opening it and gaining the mysterious power from the ancient times, Esmeralda leaves off to the Minos Labyrinth to find secrets of the Starmoon Casket.

But unfortunately, on her way to the Minos Labyrinth, the ground suddenly shakes and the desert falls into pieces, followed by a black sandstorm. All of a sudden, the Starmoon Casket opens and a stream of mysterious power fills in Esmeralda. Under the protection of the mysterious power, Esmeralda gets no harm and feels old memories flashing back through her mind.

She feels the Starmoon Casket is calling for her, reminding her that Khufra, the ruthless tyrant of the Western Desert long ago, has recovered himself. Following the lead of the Starmoon Casket, Esmeralda changes direction to the deep desert, where there stands an ancient city.

After the Starmoon Casket opens, two streams of power coming from it begin centering around Esmeralda - Frostmoon, enriched with magic, and Stardust, filled with great power. Impressed by the might of the Starmoon Casket, which was only spoken of in tales before, Esmeralda recalls the memories from the first generation of astrologers.

When Esmeralda approaches the ancient city, she vaguely feels an unknown love story between Khufra and some astrologers. Seeing the end of their love, Esmeralda somehow feels sad and sorry. But it's her mission to seal Khufra and return the peace to the Western Desert.

details.markdown:
Esmeralda manipulates Stardust and Frostmoon: Stardust deals Physical Damage while Frostmoon deals Magic Damage and gives the target a shield equal to 135% of Frostmoon's damage dealt. Each Basic Attack deals Physical and Magic damage as separate components. The damage dealt by Esmeralda ignores all shield effects and gradually converts her shield into HP, with conversion amount based on level and stats.

Notes:
- Frostmoon's shield to enemies is a debuff, therefore, healing increases (such as blessings) and healing reductions will not be applied to those, and it can also be removed by certain dispel effects.
- Frostmoon's shield for enemies can be stacked up, which is unlimited and lasts for 5 seconds. Its duration is reset by inflicting damage by Frostmoon.

Esmeralda gains a shield and 40% Movement Speed which rapidly decays over its duration for 4 seconds. Meanwhile, she gradually transforms the shields of her nearby enemies into her own shield, with the conversion amount based on level and stats, capped at 50% of her Max HP.

Base Shield: 30 / 390 / 480 / 570 / 660 / 750

Effects:
- Grants a temporary shield.
- Increases Movement Speed for 4 seconds, decaying over time.
- Steals and converts nearby enemies' shields into her own, up to 50% of her Max HP.

Esmeralda waves Stardust and Frostmoon, dealing Physical and Magic damage to nearby enemies. Each time she deals damage to a hero, the cooldown of Frostmoon Shield is reduced by 1.5 seconds. Damaging non-hero units grants a 0.5 second reduction. The enemy's Movement Speed is reduced by 10% for 1.5 second.

Base Physical Damage: 240 / 280 / 320 / 360 / 400 / 440
Added Magic Damage: 300 / 350 / 400 / 450 / 500 / 550

Effects:
- Area-of-effect Physical and Magic damage around Esmeralda.
- Reduces Frostmoon Shield cooldown when hitting enemies.
- Slows enemies hit by 10% for 1.5 second.

Notes:
- When an enemy hero and a creep are both hit, the cooldown reduction when hitting an enemy hero takes priority and they do not stack.

Esmeralda coheres the power of Astrospace into her two weapons - Stardust and Frostmoon. The longer this skill is channeled, the longer the cast range.

Activation: Esmeralda casts the Stardust at the target location, dealing Physical damage based on level and stats, and then blinks to target location and casts Frostmoon, dealing Magic damage based on level and stats and immobilizing enemies in the area for 1 second.

Base Physical Damage: 350 / 500 / 650
Added Magic Damage: 350 / 500 / 650

Effects:
- Chargeable leap and strike ultimate.
- Deals both Physical and Magic burst damage in an area.
- Immobilizes enemies hit for 1 second.

Notes:
- This skill can be charged up to 1.5 seconds and can be overcharged for another 3 seconds.
- If this skill is interrupted or manually cancelled, the mana cost will be refunded.

trivia.markdown: The name Esmeralda is of Spanish and Portuguese origin, meaning "emerald" (the precious green gemstone). It comes from the Spanish word esmeralda, which itself is derived from the Greek smaragdos, meaning "green gem."
Her name complements her Starmoon Casket passive, representing the combination of the stars (Stardust) and the moon (Frostmoon).

Esmeralda's name and character design are somewhat inspired by the character with the same name in the 1996 Disney film The Hunchback of Notre Dame, which itself is an adaptation of the 1831 novel by Victor Hugo.
Esmeralda is one of the heroes whose active skills can cast in motion using joystick.
Esmeralda is the only hero who can give shield to enemy.
Esmeralda wears golden jewelry, including bangles, earrings, and a headpiece, which resemble traditional Middle Eastern fashion particularly from Egypt.
Her skin name "Cleopatra" is based on queen Cleopatra of Egypt.
Her skin "Poison Vine" is based on Poison Ivy from the DC Universe.
Alongside Khufra, she has the first voice lines that can only be activated by interacting with a specific opposing hero (Khufra in this case.)
Currently, Esmeralda and at least one other hero are the only heroes in the game classified as both Mage and Tank.

markdown:
# Profile

**Full Name:** Esmeralda

**Alias:** Astrologer

**Born:** Stargate Valley, Agelta Drylands

**Species:** Human

**Gender:** Female

**Occupation:** Astrologer

**Affiliation:** Agelta astrologers

**Weapons:**
- Stardust
- Frostmoon

**Abilities:**
- Shield through the power of the Starmoon Casket
- Astrospace power

Born into a family of Agelta astrologers, she is the greatest astrologers on the Land of Dawn.

Through the treasure star-moon box, she learned the fact that the tyrant Khufra was about to wake up. The re-sealing of Khufra became her new mission as an astrologer.

# Story

"Mysterious astrologer"

The astrologers, who have constantly been observing astrospace, possess the occult abilities to explain and predict everything in the world, including both about their processes and results. To become an astrologer, candidates should pass rigorous assessments, which will test both their willpower and astrological abilities. Going down the generations, the elders finally select Esmeralda, a young girl living in the Western Desert, to be a new successor in the Land of Dawn.

After becoming the successor of the astrologers, Esmeralda guards the Starmoon Casket that has been passed down for generations. With the hope of opening it and gaining the mysterious power from the ancient times, Esmeralda leaves off to the Minos Labyrinth to find secrets of the Starmoon Casket.

But unfortunately, on her way to the Minos Labyrinth, the ground suddenly shakes and the desert falls into pieces, followed by a black sandstorm. All of a sudden, the Starmoon Casket opens and a stream of mysterious power fills in Esmeralda. Under the protection of the mysterious power, Esmeralda gets no harm and feels old memories flashing back through her mind.

She feels the Starmoon Casket is calling for her, reminding her that Khufra, the ruthless tyrant of the Western Desert long ago, has recovered himself. Following the lead of the Starmoon Casket, Esmeralda changes direction to the deep desert, where there stands an ancient city.

After the Starmoon Casket opens, two streams of power coming from it begin centering around Esmeralda - Frostmoon, enriched with magic, and Stardust, filled with great power. Impressed by the might of the Starmoon Casket, which was only spoken of in tales before, Esmeralda recalls the memories from the first generation of astrologers.

When Esmeralda approaches the ancient city, she vaguely feels an unknown love story between Khufra and some astrologers. Seeing the end of their love, Esmeralda somehow feels sad and sorry. But it's her mission to seal Khufra and return the peace to the Western Desert.

# Abilities

## Starmoon Casket

Esmeralda manipulates Stardust and Frostmoon: Stardust deals Physical Damage while Frostmoon deals Magic Damage and gives the target a shield equal to 135% of Frostmoon's damage dealt. Each Basic Attack deals Physical and Magic damage as separate components. The damage dealt by Esmeralda ignores all shield effects and gradually converts her shield into HP, with conversion amount based on level and stats.

Notes:
- Frostmoon's shield to enemies is a debuff, therefore, healing increases (such as blessings) and healing reductions will not be applied to those, and it can also be removed by certain dispel effects.
- Frostmoon's shield for enemies can be stacked up, which is unlimited and lasts for 5 seconds. Its duration is reset by inflicting damage by Frostmoon.

## Frostmoon Shield

Esmeralda gains a shield and 40% Movement Speed which rapidly decays over its duration for 4 seconds. Meanwhile, she gradually transforms the shields of her nearby enemies into her own shield, with the conversion amount based on level and stats, capped at 50% of her Max HP.

Base Shield: 30 / 390 / 480 / 570 / 660 / 750

Effects:
- Grants a temporary shield.
- Increases Movement Speed for 4 seconds, decaying over time.
- Steals and converts nearby enemies' shields into her own, up to 50% of her Max HP.

## Stardust Dance

Esmeralda waves Stardust and Frostmoon, dealing Physical and Magic damage to nearby enemies. Each time she deals damage to a hero, the cooldown of Frostmoon Shield is reduced by 1.5 seconds. Damaging non-hero units grants a 0.5 second reduction. The enemy's Movement Speed is reduced by 10% for 1.5 second.

Base Physical Damage: 240 / 280 / 320 / 360 / 400 / 440
Added Magic Damage: 300 / 350 / 400 / 450 / 500 / 550

Effects:
- Area-of-effect Physical and Magic damage around Esmeralda.
- Reduces Frostmoon Shield cooldown when hitting enemies.
- Slows enemies hit by 10% for 1.5 second.

Notes:
- When an enemy hero and a creep are both hit, the cooldown reduction when hitting an enemy hero takes priority and they do not stack.

## Falling Starmoon

Esmeralda coheres the power of Astrospace into her two weapons - Stardust and Frostmoon. The longer this skill is channeled, the longer the cast range.

Activation: Esmeralda casts the Stardust at the target location, dealing Physical damage based on level and stats, and then blinks to target location and casts Frostmoon, dealing Magic damage based on level and stats and immobilizing enemies in the area for 1 second.

Base Physical Damage: 350 / 500 / 650
Added Magic Damage: 350 / 500 / 650

Effects:
- Chargeable leap and strike ultimate.
- Deals both Physical and Magic burst damage in an area.
- Immobilizes enemies hit for 1 second.

Notes:
- This skill can be charged up to 1.5 seconds and can be overcharged for another 3 seconds.
- If this skill is interrupted or manually cancelled, the mana cost will be refunded.

# Trivia

- The name Esmeralda is of Spanish and Portuguese origin, meaning "emerald" (the precious green gemstone). It comes from the Spanish word esmeralda, which itself is derived from the Greek smaragdos, meaning "green gem."
- Her name complements her Starmoon Casket passive, representing the combination of the stars (Stardust) and the moon (Frostmoon).
- Esmeralda's name and character design are somewhat inspired by the character with the same name in the 1996 Disney film The Hunchback of Notre Dame, which itself is an adaptation of the 1831 novel by Victor Hugo.
- Esmeralda is one of the heroes whose active skills can cast in motion using joystick.
- Esmeralda is the only hero who can give shield to enemy.
- Esmeralda wears golden jewelry, including bangles, earrings, and a headpiece, which resemble traditional Middle Eastern fashion particularly from Egypt.
- Her skin name "Cleopatra" is based on queen Cleopatra of Egypt.
- Her skin "Poison Vine" is based on Poison Ivy from the DC Universe.
- Alongside Khufra, she has the first voice lines that can only be activated by interacting with a specific opposing hero (Khufra in this case.)
- Currently, Esmeralda and at least one other hero are the only heroes in the game classified as both Mage and Tank.

metadata.hook: She feels the Starmoon Casket is calling for her, reminding her that Khufra, the ruthless tyrant of the Western Desert long ago, has recovered himself.
metadata.teaser: Chosen as the astrologers' successor, Esmeralda unlocks the Starmoon Casket and must face the revived desert tyrant Khufra to restore peace.
